prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene in a 3Blue1Brown-inspired explanatory style. Start with a grid and two basis vectors, then apply a linear transformation matrix and narrate how the whole plane changes while the basis vectors explain the motion.
completion:
from manim import *

class LinearTransformationStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        matrix = [[1.2, 0.6], [-0.2, 1.1]]
        title = Text("A Matrix Explains a Whole Motion", font_size=36).to_edge(UP)
        plane = NumberPlane(x_range=[-5, 5, 1], y_range=[-3.5, 3.5, 1], background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.32}).shift(DOWN * 0.15)
        i_hat = Vector([1, 0], color=GREEN_D)
        j_hat = Vector([0, 1], color=RED_D)
        point = Dot(plane.c2p(2, 1), color=YELLOW, radius=0.08)
        point_label = MathTex("v", color=YELLOW).next_to(point, UR, buff=0.12)
        matrix_mob = Matrix(matrix).scale(0.72).to_corner(UR).shift(DOWN * 0.55)
        caption_a = Text("Two basis vectors are enough to describe how the entire grid will move.", font_size=24, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("Once the basis changes, every point inherits the same linear rule.", font_size=24, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("The matrix is compact, but its effect spreads across the whole plane.", font_size=24, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        i_box = SurroundingRectangle(i_hat, color=GREEN_D, buff=0.08)
        j_box = SurroundingRectangle(j_hat, color=RED_D, buff=0.08)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(plane), run_time=4)
        self.play(GrowArrow(i_hat), GrowArrow(j_hat), FadeIn(point), FadeIn(point_label), FadeIn(matrix_mob), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(Create(i_box), run_time=1.5)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(i_box, j_box), run_time=1.5)
        self.play(FadeOut(j_box), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=1.5)
        self.play(ApplyMatrix(matrix, VGroup(plane, i_hat, j_hat, point, point_label)), run_time=7)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), run_time=2)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene in a 3Blue1Brown-inspired style that explains how a secant line turns into a tangent line. Use a moving dx tracker, clear labels, and staged captions that walk from average change to instantaneous change.
completion:
from manim import *

class SecantTangentStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        def f(x):
            return 0.14 * (x - 3.4) ** 3 - 0.7 * (x - 3.4) + 2.2

        x0 = 3.4
        dx = ValueTracker(1.4)
        title = Text("Average Change Becomes Instantaneous Change", font_size=35).to_edge(UP)
        axes = Axes(x_range=[0, 7, 1], y_range=[0, 4.6, 1], x_length=8.2, y_length=4.8, tips=False).shift(DOWN * 0.2)
        graph = axes.plot(f, x_range=[0.4, 6.6], color=BLUE_D)
        base_dot = Dot(axes.c2p(x0, f(x0)), color=YELLOW, radius=0.08)
        move_dot = always_redraw(lambda: Dot(axes.c2p(x0 + dx.get_value(), f(x0 + dx.get_value())), color=TEAL_D, radius=0.08))
        secant = always_redraw(lambda: Line(base_dot.get_center(), move_dot.get_center(), color=ORANGE, stroke_width=5))
        dx_brace = always_redraw(lambda: BraceBetweenPoints(axes.c2p(x0, 0), axes.c2p(x0 + dx.get_value(), 0), direction=DOWN))
        dx_label = always_redraw(lambda: MathTex("dx", color=ORANGE).scale(0.9).next_to(dx_brace, DOWN, buff=0.08))
        slope_label = always_redraw(lambda: MathTex("\\frac{df}{dx}", color=ORANGE).scale(0.9).next_to(secant.get_center(), UP, buff=0.14))
        tangent = always_redraw(lambda: self._tangent_segment(axes, f, x0))
        caption_a = Text("With a visible gap, the secant line measures average change.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("As dx shrinks, the second point slides toward the first.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("In the limit, the secant settles into the tangent direction.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axes), Create(graph), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(base_dot), FadeIn(move_dot), Create(secant), FadeIn(dx_brace), FadeIn(dx_label), FadeIn(slope_label), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(dx.animate.set_value(0.9), run_time=4)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), dx.animate.set_value(0.35), run_time=5)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), FadeIn(tangent), dx.animate.set_value(0.08), run_time=5)
        self.wait(2)

    def _tangent_segment(self, axes, f, x_value, width=1.15):
        slope = (f(x_value + 0.02) - f(x_value - 0.02)) / 0.04
        center = axes.c2p(x_value, f(x_value))
        dx = width / (2 * np.sqrt(1 + slope**2))
        dy = slope * dx
        return Line(center + np.array([-dx, -dy, 0]), center + np.array([dx, dy, 0]), color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=6)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene in a 3Blue1Brown-inspired style that starts with a handful of discrete sample bars and gradually suggests a smooth curve passing through the same trend. Make the scene feel like an explanatory reveal rather than a dashboard.
completion:
from manim import *

class DiscreteToCurveStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Discrete Pieces Can Hint at a Smooth Law", font_size=35).to_edge(UP)
        axes = Axes(x_range=[0, 8, 1], y_range=[0, 3.2, 0.5], x_length=8.2, y_length=4.8, tips=False).shift(DOWN * 0.2)
        sample_x = [0.8, 1.8, 2.8, 3.8, 4.8, 5.8, 6.8]
        sample_y = [0.55, 1.0, 1.65, 2.2, 2.45, 2.05, 1.35]
        bars = VGroup()
        dots = VGroup()
        for x, y in zip(sample_x, sample_y, strict=True):
            width = axes.c2p(0.7, 0)[0] - axes.c2p(0, 0)[0]
            height = axes.c2p(0, y)[1] - axes.c2p(0, 0)[1]
            bar = Rectangle(width=width, height=height, stroke_color=BLUE_D, fill_color=BLUE_D, fill_opacity=0.22)
            bar.move_to(axes.c2p(x, y / 2))
            bars.add(bar)
            dots.add(Dot(axes.c2p(x, y), color=YELLOW, radius=0.07))
        smooth_curve = axes.plot(lambda x: float(np.interp(x, sample_x, sample_y)), x_range=[sample_x[0], sample_x[-1]], color=TEAL_D)
        area = axes.get_area(smooth_curve, x_range=(sample_x[0], sample_x[-1]), color=TEAL_D, opacity=0.18)
        caption_a = Text("At first we only see separate chunks of information.", font_size=24, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("The dots suggest a pattern that the bars alone only hint at.", font_size=24, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("A smooth curve turns those pieces into one continuous story.", font_size=24, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axes), run_time=4)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[GrowFromEdge(bar, DOWN) for bar in bars], lag_ratio=0.14), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=5)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(dot) for dot in dots], lag_ratio=0.12), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=4)
        self.play(Create(smooth_curve), FadeIn(area), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), run_time=5)
        self.wait(2)
